reason: The node says 'the same argument works' without explicitly identifying which steps are constraint-type-independent and which require the inequality/sublevel-set version of Weis-Shirokov. A more rigorous formulation would note: (a) Step 1.3.2 (face-commutant correspondence) is entirely independent of constraint type, since it relates F_{S(A)}(phi) to the commutant and does not reference C at all; (b) Step 1.3.3 uses Weis-Shirokov, and the inequality version requires invoking Node 1.2.1 for sublevel sets (not just level sets) and Node 1.2.2 for mixed constraints; (c) Step 1.3.4 is pure algebra. Also, the inference type is listed as 'assumption' but this is actually a corollary/claim that follows from the established machinery -- it is not a hypothesis being assumed.
